zh.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux是Robert de Vaux V的兒子，於大約1175年出生在英國波藍德。他與Petronilla Craon結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1244年在他的家鄉享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux是Robert de Vaux V的儿子，于大约1175年出生在英国波藍德。他与Petronilla Craon结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1244年在他的家乡享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Oliver
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a French name, Olivier, recorded as the name of one of Charlemagne's paladins (retainers), the close companion in arms of Roland in the Chanson de Roland. Whereas Roland is headstrong and rash, Oliver is thoughtful and cautious. Ostensibly this name derives from Late Latin olivarius ‘olive tree’ ( compare Olive ), but Charlemagne's other paladins all bear solidly Germanic names, so it is more probably an altered form of a Germanic name, perhaps distantly connected with Old Norse Óleifr ‘ancestral relic’. It has remained in more or less continuous use since the medieval period, becoming ever more popular since the 1980s.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux was born about 1175, in Cumberland, England as the son of Robert de Vaux V. He married Petronilla Craon. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1244, in his hometown, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux est né environ 1175, à Cumberland, Angleterre, fils de Robert de Vaux V. Il a épousé Petronilla Craon. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1244, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux wurde um 1175 in Cumberland, England, als Sohn von Robert de Vaux V geboren. Er hat Petronilla Craon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1244 im Alter von 71 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux è nato il circa 1175 nel Cumberland, England come figlio di Robert de Vaux V. Lui sposato Petronilla Craon. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1244, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vauxは約1175年にイギリスクウンバーランドでRobert de Vaux Vの息子として生まれた。Petronilla Craonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1244年に故郷で71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux(은)는 대략 1175년 영국 c벌랜드에서 Robert de Vaux V의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Petronilla Craon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1244년 그의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux nasceu aproximadamente 1175, em Cumberland, Inglaterra filho de Robert de Vaux V. Ele casou-se com Petronilla Craon. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1244, em sua cidade natal, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux родился около 1175 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия. Его отца звали Robert de Vaux V. Он женился на женщине по имени Petronilla Craon. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1244 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 71 год.

sm.about 1175–about 1244: e tusa o le 1175–e tusa o le 1244

es.lifeSummary: Oliver de Vaux nació aproximadamente 1175, en Cumberland, Inglaterra como hijo de Robert de Vaux V. Se casó con Petronilla Craon. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1244, en su pueblo, a la edad de 71 años.
